channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Star Wars Droid Tycoon Update in Fortnite Makes Grinding Upgrade Chips Easier
item.content:encoded:
Star Wars Droid Tycoon has taken the Fortnite world by storm, and the creators are not resting on their laurels and continue to provide updates to the game, including a tweak to make grinding Upgrade Chips easier.

Upgrade Chips in Droid Tycoon are a crucial resource that are used to upgrade your Droids, without having to wait for higher rarities to spawn at the Jawa Sandcrawler, but the grind to secure the hundreds, and in some cases thousands, required to upgrade some Droids has been unforgiving.

However, a fresh patch bringing Droid Tycoon to Version 13 has changed the formula, providing another quick way to grind for Upgrade Chips.

Upgrade Chips Grind in Star Wars Droid Tycoon Made Easier

As shown in the patch details for Version 13, Star Wars Droid Tycoon now provides Upgrade Chips when selling Gold, Diamond, or Rainbow Droids.

The higher the base rarity of the Droid, the more the amount of Chips you receive for selling them, although the grind can still take a while, as the average is around four to eight Upgrade Chips per Droid.

It is still a considerable improvement to the previous Chip-farming method, however, and shows that the developer is still listening to feedback and is prepared to make considerable updates swiftly.

Upgrading some Droids still takes thousands of Upgrade Chips though, and it remains to be seen whether future updates are planned for late-game content.

Several patches have now been deployed for Droid Tycoon since the game went live, and there’s more to come with the mysterious BB8 event set to take place this weekend.

Are you enjoying Star Wars Droid Tycoon in Fortnite? Share your thoughts on the mode in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Droid Tycoon, find out how to grind Upgrade Chips, and take a look at all the Rebirth requirements.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Today’s Contexto: Hints & Answer For May 6, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Contexto is a word association game that gives you a new word to guess every single day. With every attempt, the AI algorithm determines how close your guess is to the daily answer based on thematic and contextual similarity.

Sometimes, though, no matter how experienced you are, the word you’re looking for can be incredibly vague and hard to deduce. Luckily for you, this guide is full of hints to give you a helping hand, but make sure you give today’s Contexto a go if you haven’t already!

How To Play Contexto

Contexto is incredibly simple to play, but difficult to master. All you have to do is type words into the search bar, and the AI algorithm will tell you how close your word is to the answer.

You’ll know how close your guess is based on the colour of the bar and the number next to it.

  • Red includes guesses with the number 1,501 and above, meaning you’re way off from the secret word.
  • Orange includes guesses between 301 and 1,500, meaning you’re getting closer, but still some ways off.
  • Green includes guesses between 1 and 300, meaning you’re on the right track.

Best Contexto Tips & Tricks

  • Start off using broad terms, then narrow down once you’ve got under the 1,000 mark.
  • Try using Nouns first, and dip into verbs or adjectives once you’ve discovered the context.
  • Experiment with random words at times, especially as different forms of a root word can rank differently, such as work and worker.
  • Guess away, as unlike other word games like Wordle, you aren’t limited to a strict number of attempts.

Best Starting Words For Contexto

Here are some great starting words to get you going with Contexto:

  • Thing
  • Person
  • Place
  • Animal
  • Work
  • Nature
  • Game
  • Concept
  • Idea
  • Food

Contexto Hints For Today, May 6, 2026

Without giving away today’s Contexto answer, here are a few helpful hints to get you thinking in the right direction:

  • Hint 1: Loud
  • Hint 2: Sign of illness
  • Hint 3: Impacts your throat

If they don’t help, then here are the nine closest words, going from tenth to second:

  • Runny
  • Ache
  • Sore
  • Sneeze
  • Sniffle
  • Throat
  • Wheeze
  • Bronchitis
  • Cold

Today’s Contexto Answer For May 6, 2026

If you’ve tried everything and still can’t figure out today’s Contexto answer, then you can find it down below:

Answer: Cough

A Cough is a forceful push of air that your body uses to clear irritants out of your airways.

When Is The Next Contexto Game?

Contexto is updated daily at 12 AM or Midnight in your respective time zone, meaning the next game will start on May 7.

The Contexto schedule is very consistent, with a new game releasing at the same time every day.

How did you find today’s Contexto? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

MOBILE APP

item.title: Jnyxzi LoL Streamer Tournament To Feature Mr Beast, CaseOh and More
item.content:encoded:
Twitch streamer Nicholas ‘Jynxzi’ Stewart has revealed the star-studded line-up for his content creator League of Legends tournament.

Jynxzi only recently began playing LoL and has quickly become addicted, discovering the good and bad the game has to offer.

The streamer has received the full experience, with Jynxzi receiving a threat of a LoL ban for intentionally trying to lose after dealing just 500 damage in a match.

He also had his first account permanently banned after he bought it pre-levelled, though Riot Games then promptly gifted him another.

Now, Jynxzi has decided to host a tournament made up mostly of creators who haven’t played much of the game.

The tournament will feature 40 streamers in total and will take place on Monday, May 11.

Each team will only be allowed one Challenger-ranked player, and that high-level player will be limited to playing support on a low-damage champion.

Full List of Streamers in Jynxzi LoL Tournament

So far, Jynxzi has confirmed just under 30 streamers who will participate in the 40-player tournament.

This includes some major names such as James ‘Mr Beast’ Donaldson and potentially Case Dylan ‘CaseOh’ Baker.

The tournament will also feature streamers that are also invested in LCS LoL teams, and Jeremy ‘Disguised Toast’ Wang (Disguised) as well as Shopify Rebellion co-owners Ludwig Ahren and Charlie ‘MoistCr1TiKaL’ White Jr.

Mr Beast has also previously discussed buying a LoL esports team

As for the Challenger players, former LCS pros Yiliang ‘Doublelift’ Peng and Eugene ‘Pobelter’ Park are joined by European LoL streamers Alois ‘AloisNL’ Nelissen and Frederik ‘Noway4u’ Hinteregger.

  • Jynxzi
  • CourageJD
  • Ludwig
  • Doublelift
  • Pobelter
  • MoistCritikal
  • DisguisedToast
  • MrBeast
  • LosPollos
  • Noway4u
  • Tyler1
  • Pokelawls
  • Yassuo
  • ohnePixel
  • AloisNL
  • Emiru
  • Ron
  • ArrowCS
  • Dantes
  • Willjum
  • Yusuf
  • Arky
  • Mooda
  • Ray
  • Sketch
  • Marlon
  • Kingsman
  • CaseOh (maybe)

What streamer or content creator would you want to see in the tournament? Let us know in the Insider Gaming Discord.

Meanwhile, if you’re curious as to what top-level LoL looks like, discover how to watch Korean LoL EWC qualifiers.

item.title: Five Pokémon That Would Shake Up the Pokémon Champions Meta
item.content:encoded:
At this point, the VGC doubles meta is mostly solved for Pokémon Champions and its limited roster of playable Pokémon.

Though the winner of the first Global Challenge ran the surprising choice of Ariados on his team, showing there’s still room for creativity.

While Season M-1 ends on Wednesday, May 13, there’s going to be a longer wait for new Pokémon in Pokémon Champions.

Still, here are five non-legendary Pokémon that would completely revolutionise the current meta.

Mega Staraptor

One of the most highly anticipated Pokémon is the new Mega Evolution for Staraptor, which has never been playable in traditional battles.

If the rumours are true, Mega Staraptor is going to be monstrously broken if it doesn’t receive any nerfs.

According to leaks and data miners, Mega Staraptor will receive the ability Contrary, which reverses all stat changes.

This means the Pokémon can come in and lower everyone’s attack with Intimidate, then Mega Evolve to become a part-fighting type and hit a STAB Close Combat and then increase both its defense and special defense.

With its base 140 attack and 110 speed, this Mega would immediately become one of the most powerful options in the game.

Gholdengo

Gholdengo was one of the strongest Pokémon from generation nine, and it matches up beautifully into the current Pokémon Champions meta.

Its rare typing of Steel Ghost and its Good As Gold ability making it immune to status moves makes it rather tough to take down.

With immunity to both poison and fighting-type attacks, it completely walls Sneasler, one of the most-used mons in Pokémon Champions.

Its high special attack and signature steel-type move, Make It Rain, would also allow it to delete Mega Floette, one of the strongest Pokémon in the current meta.

Rillaboom

So far, the Pokémon Champions meta has revolved around weather wars with sand, rain, sun, and snow teams all dominating the ladder.

Considering the game’s simplicity, the addition of more reliable terrain setters would completely flip the meta on its head.

And few are more reliable than Rillaboom, which can automatically set Grassy Terrain with its Grassy Surge ability.

This terrain restores every grounded Pokémon’s HP slightly, boosts grass attacks by 50%, and weakens Earthquake and similar moves.

Bulky Rillaboom benefits from the heals and increased damage, especially with the powerful priority move Glassy Glide which lets it effectively counter Trick Room and Tailwind.

Amoongus

Another Grass type, Amoongus has long been a staple of competitive Pokémon.

While Sinistcha has been plaguing the Pokémon Champions ladder, Amoongus may outclass it with its ridiculous bulk.

Amoongus is very difficult to kill, especially with its Regenerator ability, which heals it every time it switches out.

And it has access to very powerful moves in VGC, such as Rage Powder and Spore, making it an excellent utility pick.

Galarian Weezing

Finally, Galarian Weezing would add some very necessary counterplay to Pokémon Champions.

Its ability Neutralizing Gas, unique to the Weezing line, stops the abilities of all Pokémon while it’s on the field.

This would be absolutely massive in the current game state, especially as there is currently no answer to Mega Scovillain’s Spicy Spray ability, which burns every Pokémon that attacks it.

Shutting down other abilities like Intimidate, Archuladon’s Stamina, and Mega Meganium’s Mega Sol could be game-winning.

Plus, Galarian Weezing’s Poison Fairy typing and impressive bulk make it a very useful pick.

What Pokémon do you want to see in Pokémon Champions? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

And discover how to get more Pokémon Champions team slots without paying for a membership.
